basics.name: Kaouter Essakkat
basics.summary: Postdoctoral Research Associate at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. Research in environmental and resource economics, technology adoption, and behavioral responses to economic incentives.

location.address: Institute for Sustainability, Energy, and Environment, University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign

work.name: University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
work.summary: Institute for Sustainability, Energy, and Environment (iSEE).
highlights: Lead research on adoption of AI-based technologies in agricultural systems under environmental externalities.
Develop a coupled human–natural systems framework linking discrete choice models with spatial agent-based models.
Analyze how heterogeneous preferences, peer effects, and ecological spillovers shape adoption and environmental outcomes.

work.name: University of Nebraska-Lincoln
highlights: Designed and implemented a large-scale discrete choice experiment to study producer valuation of soil health-related ecosystem services.
Estimated willingness to pay and preference heterogeneity using mixed logit and latent class models.
Analyzed geographic location preferences and implications for policy design.
Examined adoption of conservation practices using spatial econometric methods.

selected presentations.name: Spatial-Dynamic Adoption of AI Weeding Robots
Dynamic Adoption of AI Weeding Robots
From Data to Decisions: Modeling Farmer Adoption of AI Weed Technology
Exploring the Determinants of Cover Crop Adoption
